title: See total number of countries in the country column, using a dashboard widget
excerpt: It would be very useful if it could be possible to see the total number of countries you have in the country column using the dashboard widget.

posts.author: GustavoM (J. Gustavo M)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be very useful if it could be possible to see the total number of countries you have in the country column using the dashboard widget.

posts.author: david_eshop (David OBrien)
posts.cookedHtml: It might not look as nice, but you could always try to use the Pivot table widget. This should give you the numbers that you are looking for, for each country.

posts.author: GustavoM (J. Gustavo M)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks David… I tried and yes… the pivot table does not look as nice for a dashboard as other widgets, but at least in the meanwhile that we wait for a nicer solution from monday.com team, it is a work around that makes it possible to see the total number of countries in a board setting the pivot table to “Count Unique Values” of the Country column.

posts.author: vikpaw (Vik Paw)
posts.cookedHtml:
Did this work okay for you? For some reason my count is out by 1. I think it’s counting blanks. There aren’t any blank rows, but there are subitems with blanks and it seems to be confused.
Annoying that we’re using a workaround that isn’t pretty but now the count is out. Not sure if there’s a resolution.
